261	// checkForUpdates performs automatic update check (synchronous, once per 24h)
262	func (f *CommandFactory) checkForUpdates(cmd *cobra.Command) {
263		// Check if disabled via env var
264		if os.Getenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK") == "1" {
265			return
266		}
267	
268		// Load config
269		cfg, err := config.Load()
270		if err != nil {
271			// Silent failure - use defaults
272			cfg = config.DefaultNewConfig()
273		}
274	
275		// Check if disabled via config
276		if cfg.UpdateCheck != nil && !cfg.UpdateCheck.Enabled {
277			return
278		}
279	
280		// Create checker and perform check (synchronous)
281		checker := update.NewChecker(f.Version, cfg)
282		f.updateMu.Lock()
283		f.updateChecker = checker
284		f.updateDone = make(chan struct{})
285		done := f.updateDone
286		f.updateMu.Unlock()
287	
288		// Run the check asynchronously so command startup is never blocked.
289		go func() {
290			defer close(done)
291			_, _ = checker.CheckForUpdate(context.Background())
292			// Also refresh plugin version cache while we have a network call in flight.
293			f.refreshPluginVersionCache()
294		}()
295	}

297	// displayUpdateNotification shows update notification if available
298	func (f *CommandFactory) displayUpdateNotification(cmd *cobra.Command) error {
299		// Skip if disabled via environment variable
300		if os.Getenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK") == "1" {
301			return nil
302		}
303	
304		f.updateMu.Lock()
305		checker := f.updateChecker
306		done := f.updateDone
307		f.updateMu.Unlock()
308	
309		if checker == nil {
310			return nil
311		}
312	
313		if done != nil {
314			select {
315			case <-done:
316			default:
317				return nil
318			}
319		}
320	
321		// Skip update notification for help commands
322		if cmd.Name() == "help" || cmd.Parent() != nil && cmd.Parent().Name() == "help" {
323			return nil
324		}
325	
326		// Skip update notification for machine-readable output formats
327		jsonFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
328		if jsonFlag {
329			return nil
330		}
331	
332		// Skip update notification if silent flag is set
333		silentFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("silent")
334		if silentFlag {
335			return nil
336		}
337	
338		// Skip update notification if --no-check flag is set (for version command)
339		noCheck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-check")
340		if noCheck {
341			return nil
342		}
343	
344		// Skip when stdout is not a terminal (pipes, scripts, CI)
345		if !isatty.IsTerminal(os.Stdout.Fd()) && !isatty.IsCygwinTerminal(os.Stdout.Fd()) {
346			return nil
347		}
348	
349		available, version, err := checker.IsUpdateAvailable()
350		if err != nil || !available {
351			if err != nil {
352				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: Could not check for updates: %v\n", err)
353			}
354			return nil
355		}
356	
357		// Show update notification with changelog for version command
358		// Always write to stderr so it doesn't corrupt JSON pipelines
359		isVersionCmd := cmd.Use == "version"
360		if isVersionCmd {
361			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr,
362				"\n⬆️  Update available: %s (run 'ddx upgrade' to install)\n\nWhat's new:\n  • Performance improvements\n  • Bug fixes\n  • New features\n",
363				version)
364		} else {
365			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr,
366				"\n⬆️  Update available: %s (run 'ddx upgrade' to install)\n",
367				version)
368		}
369	
370		return nil
371	}

373	// checkVersionGate blocks execution if the binary is older than the project requires.
374	// Returns an error for non-exempt commands when binary version < project's ddx_version.
375	func (f *CommandFactory) checkVersionGate(cmd *cobra.Command) error {
376		// Dev builds bypass the gate
377		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
378			return nil
379		}
380	
381		// Read project versions
382		projectVersion := readProjectVersions(f.WorkingDir)
383		if projectVersion == "" || projectVersion == "dev" {
384			return nil // No versions.yaml or dev project — skip
385		}
386	
387		// Exempt commands that must work even when binary is too old
388		switch cmd.Name() {
389		case "upgrade", "version", "doctor", "init", "help", "completion":
390			return nil
391		}
392	
393		// Compare: is the binary older than what the project requires?
394		needsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(f.Version, projectVersion)
395		if err != nil {
396			return nil // Parse error — don't block
397		}
398		if needsUpgrade {
399			return fmt.Errorf("this project requires DDx v%s or newer (you have %s).\nRun 'ddx upgrade' to update your DDx binary",
400				strings.TrimPrefix(projectVersion, "v"),
401				f.Version)
402		}
403	
404		return nil
405	}

412		}
413		if silentFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("silent"); silentFlag {

425	
426		// Check project staleness
427		projectVersion := readProjectVersions(f.WorkingDir)
428		if projectVersion != "" && projectVersion != "dev" {
429			// Is the binary newer than the project?
430			projectNeedsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(projectVersion, f.Version)
431			if err == nil && projectNeedsUpgrade {
432				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(),

434					strings.TrimPrefix(projectVersion, "v"),

443	// displayPluginStalenessHints reads the plugin version cache and shows hints for
444	// any installed plugins that have a newer version available.
445	func (f *CommandFactory) displayPluginStalenessHints(cmd *cobra.Command) {
446		state, err := registry.LoadState()
447		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
448			return
449		}
450	
451		cache := update.NewPluginCache()
452		if err := cache.Load(); err != nil {
453			return // no cache yet — check runs in background, hints appear next time
454		}
455	
456		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
457			latestVersion, ok := cache.Data.Plugins[entry.Name]
458			if !ok {
459				continue
460			}
461			needsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(entry.Version, latestVersion)
462			if err == nil && needsUpgrade {
463				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(),
464					"\n💡 %s %s installed, %s available. Run 'ddx install %s' to update.\n",
465					entry.Name, entry.Version, latestVersion, entry.Name)
466			}
467		}
468	}

470	// refreshPluginVersionCache fetches the latest version for each installed plugin
471	// from GitHub and writes the result to the plugin cache. Called from the
472	// background update goroutine — failures are silent.
473	func (f *CommandFactory) refreshPluginVersionCache() {
474		cache := update.NewPluginCache()
475		_ = cache.Load() // ignore missing-file error
476	
477		if !cache.IsExpired() {
478			return
479		}
480	
481		state, err := registry.LoadState()
482		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
483			return
484		}
485	
486		reg := registry.BuiltinRegistry()
487		updated := false
488		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
489			pkg, err := reg.Find(entry.Name)
490			if err != nil {
491				continue
492			}
493			release, err := update.FetchLatestReleaseForRepo(pkg.Source)
494			if err != nil {
495				continue
496			}
497			latestVersion := strings.TrimPrefix(release.TagName, "v")
498			cache.Data.Plugins[entry.Name] = latestVersion
499			updated = true
500		}
501	
502		if updated {
503			cache.Data.LastCheck = time.Now()
504			_ = cache.Save()
505		}
506	}

616	var ddxBinaryStalenessPathspecs = []string{
617		"Makefile",
618		"install.sh",
619		"cli",
620	}
621	
622	func (s sourceCheckoutBinaryStaleness) recoveryCommand() string {
623		return fmt.Sprintf("cd %s && make install", s.ProjectRoot)
624	}
625	
626	func (s sourceCheckoutBinaryStaleness) warningMessage() string {
627		return fmt.Sprintf(
628			"WARNING: installed ddx is built from %s; source tree HEAD is %s (%s commits ahead).\nproject root: %s\nbinary commit: %s\nsource HEAD: %s\ninstalled ddx binary is behind this DDx source checkout.\nrecovery: %s\nRun \"make install\" to refresh.\n",
629			s.BinaryCommit,
630			s.SourceHead,
631			s.AheadCount,
632			s.ProjectRoot,
633			s.BinaryCommit,
634			s.SourceHead,
635			s.recoveryCommand(),
636		)
637	}

639	func (f *CommandFactory) detectInstalledBinaryBehindSource(projectRoot string) *sourceCheckoutBinaryStaleness {
640		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
641			return nil
642		}
643	
644		buildCommit := normalizeVersionCommit(f.Commit)
645		if buildCommit == "" {
646			return nil
647		}
648	
649		repoRoot := strings.TrimSpace(projectRoot)
650		if repoRoot == "" {
651			return nil
652		}
653		if !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoRoot) {
654			repoRoot = gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(repoRoot)
655		}
656		if repoRoot == "" || !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoRoot) {
657			return nil
658		}
659	
660		originURL, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "remote", "get-url", "origin")
661		if err != nil || !isDDXOriginURL(originURL) {
662			return nil
663		}
664	
665		buildSHA, err := gitResolveCommit(repoRoot, buildCommit)
666		if err != nil {
667			return nil
668		}
669		headSHA, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
670		if err != nil || buildSHA == headSHA {
671			return nil
672		}
673	
674		if err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), repoRoot, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", buildSHA, headSHA).Run(); err != nil {
675			return nil
676		}
677	
678		aheadCount, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-list", "--count", buildSHA+".."+headSHA)
679		if err != nil || aheadCount == "0" {
680			return nil
681		}
682		if !hasDDXBinaryAffectingDiff(repoRoot, buildSHA, headSHA) {
683			return nil
684		}
685	
686		return &sourceCheckoutBinaryStaleness{
687			ProjectRoot:  repoRoot,
688			BinaryCommit: buildSHA,
689			SourceHead:   headSHA,
690			AheadCount:   aheadCount,
691		}
692	}
693	
694	func (f *CommandFactory) warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd *cobra.Command) {
695		repoRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
696		staleness := f.detectInstalledBinaryBehindSource(repoRoot)
697		if staleness == nil {
698			return
699		}
700		_, _ = fmt.Fprint(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), staleness.warningMessage())
701	}
702	
703	func hasDDXBinaryAffectingDiff(repoRoot, baseSHA, headSHA string) bool {
704		args := []string{"diff", "--name-only", baseSHA + ".." + headSHA, "--"}
705		args = append(args, ddxBinaryStalenessPathspecs...)
706		out, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, args...)
707		return err == nil && strings.TrimSpace(out) != ""
708	}
709	
710	func gitCommandOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
711		out, err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).Output()
712		if err != nil {
713			return "", err
714		}
715		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
716	}
717	
718	func gitResolveCommit(dir, commit string) (string, error) {
719		return gitCommandOutput(dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", commit+"^{commit}")
720	}
721	
722	func isDDXOriginURL(url string) bool {
723		return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(url), "documentdrivendx/ddx")
724	}

726	// resolveAgentSession looks up a session by ID from the agent session log.
727	// Returns nil if the session is not found or cannot be read.
728	func (f *CommandFactory) resolveAgentSession(sessionID string) *agent.SessionEntry {
729		if sessionID == "" {
730			return nil
731		}
732	
733		cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(f.WorkingDir)
734		if err != nil {
735			return nil
736		}
737	
738		sessionLogDir := ".ddx/agent-logs"
739		if cfg.Agent != nil && cfg.Agent.SessionLogDir != "" {
740			sessionLogDir = cfg.Agent.SessionLogDir
741		}
742	
743		logDir := agent.ResolveLogDir(f.WorkingDir, sessionLogDir)
744		idx, ok, err := agent.FindSessionIndex(logDir, sessionID)
745		if err != nil {
746			return nil
747		}
748		if ok {
749			entry := agent.SessionIndexEntryToLegacy(idx)
750			bodies := agent.LoadSessionBodies(f.WorkingDir, idx)
751			entry.Prompt = bodies.Prompt
752			entry.Response = bodies.Response
753			entry.Stderr = bodies.Stderr
754			return &entry
755		}
756		return nil
757	}

200	// user content outside the DDx markers and updates content between them.
201	func TestGenerateAgentsMD_MergesWithMarkers(t *testing.T) {
202		workingDir := t.TempDir()

204	
205		// Pre-seed AGENTS.md with user content both before and after the DDx block
206		userBefore := "# My Project\n\nUser content before the DDx block.\n\n"
207		oldDdxBlock := agentsMarkerStart + "\nold ddx content\n" + agentsMarkerEnd + "\n"
208		userAfter := "\n## More User Content\n\nUser content after the DDx block.\n"
209		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(agentsPath, []byte(userBefore+oldDdxBlock+userAfter), 0644))

211		generateAgentsMD(workingDir)
212	
213		data, err := os.ReadFile(agentsPath)
214		require.NoError(t, err)
215		content := string(data)

217		// User content outside markers must survive
218		assert.Contains(t, content, "User content before the DDx block.", "pre-block user content lost")
219		assert.Contains(t, content, "User content after the DDx block.", "post-block user content lost")
220		// New DDx block content must be present
221		assert.Contains(t, content, "This project uses [DDx]", "new DDx block content not injected")
222		// Block markers must still exist (exactly one pair)

227		generateAgentsMD(workingDir)

229		require.NoError(t, err)
230		content2 := string(data2)
231		assert.Equal(t, 1, countOccurrences(content2, agentsMarkerStart), "re-run duplicated start marker")

269	// countOccurrences is a small test helper; we avoid strings.Count import noise here.
270	func countOccurrences(s, sub string) int {
271		count := 0
272		start := 0
273		for {
274			idx := indexFrom(s, sub, start)
275			if idx == -1 {
276				return count
277			}
278			count++
279			start = idx + len(sub)
280		}
281	}
282	
283	func indexFrom(s, sub string, start int) int {
284		if start > len(s) {
285			return -1
286		}
287		rest := s[start:]
288		for i := 0; i+len(sub) <= len(rest); i++ {
289			if rest[i:i+len(sub)] == sub {
290				return start + i
291			}
292		}
293		return -1
294	}

296	// TestInitCommand tests the init command
297	func TestInitCommand(t *testing.T) {
298		tests := []struct {
299			name        string
300			args        []string
301			envOptions  []TestEnvOption
302			setup       func(t *testing.T, te *TestEnvironment)
303			validate    func(t *testing.T, te *TestEnvironment, output string, err error)
304			expectError bool
305		}{
306			{
307				name:       "basic initialization",
308				args:       []string{"init", "--no-git"},
309				envOptions: []TestEnvOption{WithGitInit(false)},
310				validate: func(t *testing.T, te *TestEnvironment, output string, cmdErr error) {
311					// Check .ddx/config.yaml was created
312					assert.FileExists(t, te.ConfigPath)
313	
314					// Verify config content
315					data, err := os.ReadFile(te.ConfigPath)
316					require.NoError(t, err)
317	
318					var config map[string]interface{}
319					err = yaml.Unmarshal(data, &config)
320					require.NoError(t, err)
321	
322					assert.Contains(t, config, "version")
323					assert.Contains(t, config, "library")
324					if library, ok := config["library"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
325						assert.Contains(t, library, "repository")
326					}
327				},
328				expectError: false,
329			},
330			{
331				name:       "init with force flag when config exists",
332				args:       []string{"init", "--force", "--no-git"},
333				envOptions: []TestEnvOption{WithGitInit(false)},
334				setup: func(t *testing.T, te *TestEnvironment) {
335					// Create existing config in new format
336					existingConfig := `version: "0.9"
337	library:
338	  path: "./library"
339	  repository:
340	    url: "https://old.repo"
341	    branch: "main"
342	persona_bindings: {}
343	`
344					te.CreateConfig(existingConfig)
345				},
346				validate: func(t *testing.T, te *TestEnvironment, output string, cmdErr error) {
347					// Config should be overwritten
348					data, err := os.ReadFile(te.ConfigPath)
349					require.NoError(t, err)
350	
351					var config map[string]interface{}
352					err = yaml.Unmarshal(data, &config)
353					require.NoError(t, err)
354	
355					// With --force flag, creates new config with default version
356					assert.Equal(t, "1.0", config["version"])
357				},
358				expectError: false,
359			},
360			{
361				name:       "init without force when config exists",
362				args:       []string{"init", "--no-git"},
363				envOptions: []TestEnvOption{WithGitInit(false)},
364				setup: func(t *testing.T, te *TestEnvironment) {
365					// Create existing config
366					te.CreateConfig("version: \"1.0\"")
367				},
368				validate: func(t *testing.T, te *TestEnvironment, output string, err error) {
369					// Should fail
370					assert.Error(t, err)
371				},
372				expectError: true,
373			},
374		}
375	
376		for _, tt := range tests {
377			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
378				te := NewTestEnvironment(t, tt.envOptions...)
379	
380				if tt.setup != nil {
381					tt.setup(t, te)
382				}
383	
384				output, err := te.RunCommand(tt.args...)
385	
386				if tt.expectError {
387					assert.Error(t, err)
388				} else {
389					assert.NoError(t, err)
390				}
391	
392				if tt.validate != nil {
393					tt.validate(t, te, output, err)
394				}
395			})
396		}
397	}

645	// TestInitGitignoreRules verifies that ddx init writes the correct .gitignore rules
646	// for the tracked/ignored split: runtime scratch is ignored, execution evidence is tracked.
647	func TestInitGitignoreRules(t *testing.T) {
648		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
649		_, err := te.RunCommand("init", "--no-git")
650		require.NoError(t, err)
651	
652		gitignorePath := filepath.Join(te.Dir, ".gitignore")
653		data, err := os.ReadFile(gitignorePath)
654		require.NoError(t, err)
655		content := string(data)
656	
657		// Runtime scratch must be ignored
658		assert.Contains(t, content, ".ddx/agent-logs/", ".ddx/agent-logs/ must be ignored")
659		assert.Contains(t, content, ".ddx/attachments/", ".ddx/attachments/ must be ignored")
660		assert.Contains(t, content, ".ddx/workers/", ".ddx/workers/ must be ignored")
661		assert.Contains(t, content, ".ddx/exec-runs.d/", ".ddx/exec-runs.d/ must be ignored")
662		assert.Contains(t, content, ".ddx/server.env", ".ddx/server.env must be ignored")
663		assert.Contains(t, content, ".ddx/server/", ".ddx/server/ must be ignored")
664		assert.Contains(t, content, ".ddx/run-state.json", ".ddx/run-state.json must be ignored")
665		assert.Contains(t, content, ".ddx/run-state/", ".ddx/run-state/ must be ignored")
666		assert.Contains(t, content, ".ddx/executions/*/embedded/", "embedded runtime state must be ignored")
667	
668		// Execution evidence must be explicitly un-ignored
669		assert.Contains(t, content, "!.ddx/executions/", "executions/ directory must be un-ignored")
670		assert.Contains(t, content, "!.ddx/executions/*/prompt.md", "prompt.md must be un-ignored")
671		assert.Contains(t, content, "!.ddx/executions/*/manifest.json", "manifest.json must be un-ignored")
672		assert.Contains(t, content, "!.ddx/executions/*/result.json", "result.json must be un-ignored")
673		assert.Contains(t, content, "!.ddx/executions/*/usage.json", "usage.json must be un-ignored")
674	
675		// Verify with git check-ignore that a concrete evidence file is NOT ignored
676		// Set up a minimal git repo to run check-ignore
677		gitInit := exec.Command("git", "init", "-q")
678		gitInit.Dir = te.Dir
679		require.NoError(t, gitInit.Run())
680	
681		gitConfig1 := exec.Command("git", "config", "user.email", "test@test.com")
682		gitConfig1.Dir = te.Dir
683		require.NoError(t, gitConfig1.Run())
684		gitConfig2 := exec.Command("git", "config", "user.name", "Test")
685		gitConfig2.Dir = te.Dir
686		require.NoError(t, gitConfig2.Run())
687	
688		// git check-ignore exits 0 if ignored, 1 if not ignored
689		checkIgnore := exec.Command("git", "check-ignore", "-q", ".ddx/executions/abc123/prompt.md")
690		checkIgnore.Dir = te.Dir
691		err = checkIgnore.Run()
692		// exit code 1 means NOT ignored — that's what we want
693		assert.Error(t, err, ".ddx/executions/abc123/prompt.md must NOT be ignored by git")
694	
695		checkIgnoreUsage := exec.Command("git", "check-ignore", "-q", ".ddx/executions/abc123/usage.json")
696		checkIgnoreUsage.Dir = te.Dir
697		err = checkIgnoreUsage.Run()
698		// exit code 1 means NOT ignored — that's what we want
699		assert.Error(t, err, ".ddx/executions/abc123/usage.json must NOT be ignored by git")
700	}

702	func TestInitGitignoreRunStateMigration(t *testing.T) {
703		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
704		runCleanupCommandGit(t, te.Dir, "init", "-b", "main")
705		runCleanupCommandGit(t, te.Dir, "config", "user.email", "test@test.com")
706		runCleanupCommandGit(t, te.Dir, "config", "user.name", "Test")
707	
708		ddxDir := filepath.Join(te.Dir, ddxroot.DirName)
709		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "run-state"), 0o755))
710		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "run-state.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"legacy-root"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
711		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "run-state", "legacy-attempt.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"legacy-attempt"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
712		runCleanupCommandGit(t, te.Dir, "add", ".ddx/run-state.json", ".ddx/run-state/legacy-attempt.json")
713		runCleanupCommandGit(t, te.Dir, "commit", "-m", "test: track legacy run-state")
714	
715		_, err := te.RunCommand("init")
716		require.NoError(t, err)
717	
718		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(ddxDir, "run-state.json"))
719		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(ddxDir, "run-state", "legacy-attempt.json"))
720		assert.Empty(t, runCleanupCommandGit(t, te.Dir, "ls-files", "--", ".ddx/run-state.json", ".ddx/run-state"),
721			"legacy run-state files must be removed from the git index by init migration")
722		assert.Empty(t, runCleanupCommandGit(t, te.Dir, "status", "--short", "--", ".ddx/run-state.json", ".ddx/run-state"),
723			"init migration must not leave run-state deletions or modifications behind")
724	
725		data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(te.Dir, ".gitignore"))
726		require.NoError(t, err)
727		content := string(data)
728		assert.Contains(t, content, ".ddx/run-state.json")
729		assert.Contains(t, content, ".ddx/run-state/")
730	}
